title: Ishq Josir
gloss: The well-known love story of Sabz Pari, a fairy, and Gulfam, the prince of Kashmir; translated into Sindhi by B. Sanwal Singh.
notes: First SINDHI section of the year. Pokhar Das of Shikarpur (the Sindhi-market bookseller also seen in Q4) via the Arya Steam Press, Lahore.

title: Alif Leli ba Taswir
gloss: The Arabian Nights in Sindhi, with pictures.
notes: Illustrated Arabian Nights in Sindhi. Printed serial reads 3 after 1 (serial 2 not visible on this page).

flags.issue: printed serial 3 follows 1; serial 2 not present on page

title: Sindhi Majma-ul-Fawaid Tawizat Jo
gloss: A book of charms and amulets.

title: Bayan-ul-Arifin
gloss: Or an account of the famous saints of the Muhammadans; translated into Sindhi from the Persian original of Muhammad Bin Wasi.
notes: Sufi hagiography Persian→Sindhi; Sikh publisher (Hari Singh) at Sukkur.

author: Ghulam Muhammad
title: Bostan-i-Sindhi
gloss: A translation in Sindhi verse of the well known Persian book Bostan of Shaikh Sa'di.
notes: Sa'di's Bostan in Sindhi verse — the same Persian classic appears this year in Gurmukhi (Q4 p11) and via Sa'di anthologies in Urdu; Bostan/Gulistan as the pan-regional shared curriculum.

author: Hamal
title: Qisso Hir Ranjho
gloss: The well known story of Hir and Ranjha.
notes: Hir-Ranjha in Sindhi — the Punjabi classic crossing into the Sindhi market.

author: Mian Hafiz
title: Qissa Momal Maindari
gloss: An incomplete story of the love of two sisters, Momal and Maindari. Their father buried his treasure in a river which could be made dry by means of an enchanted stick, and he was thus able to take out as much as he liked. Momal once gave the stick to a beggar on which the miserly father was very angry. Maindari built a good house for her sister where they lived happily together.

title: Madah Aghisni Sharif
gloss: Verses in praise of the prophet Muhammad.
notes: All seven Sindhi entries this quarter route through Pokhar Das of Shikarpur and Lahore presses (chiefly Arya Steam) — a single Sindhi-market distribution channel (same pattern as Q4).
